Cardan Joint Fault Detection

Early Detection of Mechanical Coupling Wear

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

What is a Cardan Joint?

Cardan joints (universal joints) connect rotating shafts that are at an angle to each other. They're commonly used in industrial machinery where power must be transmitted through misaligned shafts.

When cardan joints loosen or wear, they cause vibration, noise, and eventual failure. Traditional monitoring would only catch this through visual inspection or after symptoms become obvious.

The Detection

Samotics' electrical signature analysis monitors the motor driving machinery with cardan joints. Looseness or wear in the joint creates distinctive electrical signatures:

  • Recurring vibration patterns at the joint's natural frequency
  • Irregular torque fluctuations in the motor current
  • Increased energy consumption due to mechanical friction
  • Harmonic patterns specific to joint wear

Our algorithms recognize these patterns and alert maintenance teams early.

The Benefit

By detecting cardan joint wear early, operators can schedule replacement maintenance before the joint fails and causes secondary damage to connected equipment.
